etc/seed/streams.purpose: Benthos/Redpanda Connect pipeline YAML configs that generate synthetic CloudEvents and POST them to OpenMeter's ingest API at /api/v1/events. Each file is a self-contained, infinitely-running stream parameterised entirely through environment variables.

patterns.name: CloudEvents 1.0 envelope
patterns.description: Every generated event includes id (uuid_v4()), specversion ("1.0"), type, source, subject, and time at the root; domain-specific fields go inside data: {}.

patterns.name: Env-var parameterisation with defaults
patterns.description: All tuneable values use ${ENV_VAR:default} — never hardcode intervals, counts, URLs, or tokens. Standard vars: SEEDER_INTERVAL (100ms), SEEDER_SUBJECT_COUNT (1000), OPENMETER_BASE_URL, OPENMETER_TOKEN.

patterns.name: Subject pool via modulo randomisation
patterns.description: Subjects are selected by random_int(seed: timestamp_unix_nano()) % $subject_count, formatted as "demo-subject-%d" — a bounded, repeatable subject space driven by SEEDER_SUBJECT_COUNT.

patterns.name: Dual-output switch: HTTP primary + optional stdout
patterns.description: Output is always a switch with two cases: (1) unconditional http_client POST to /api/v1/events with Content-Type application/cloudevents+json and Bearer auth, continue: true; (2) conditional stdout when SEEDER_LOG=true.
patterns.example: output: switch: cases: [ {check: "", continue: true, output: {http_client: ...}}, {check: '"${SEEDER_LOG:false}" == "true"', output: {stdout: ...}} ]

patterns.name: Infinite generation with count: 0
patterns.description: All streams use count: 0 (run forever) under input.generate — never a finite count for seed streams.

key_file_guides.role: Generates HTTP-request events (type: "request") with method and path data fields — the minimal CloudEvents + data reference.
key_file_guides.watch_for: data fields are method and path — meter definitions consuming this stream must match those exact field names.

key_file_guides.role: Generates LLM token events (type: "prompt") with tokens, model, and type (input/output) data fields.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Model strings are compared against llmcost price records; mismatches silently produce unresolved costs — keep the model list aligned with llmcost prices.

key_file_guides.role: Generates compute-workload events (type: "workload") with duration_seconds, region, zone, instance_type data fields.
key_file_guides.watch_for: zone is region+suffix (e.g. "us-east-1a"), not an independent random pick — meter group-by on zone implicitly includes region.

anti_patterns: Hardcoding OPENMETER_TOKEN or OPENMETER_BASE_URL — always use ${VAR:default}
Adding a non-CloudEvents-1.0 envelope (missing specversion, id, or time at root)
Setting count > 0 — seed streams must run indefinitely
Putting dimensional attributes outside the data: {} block
Using a fixed random seed instead of timestamp_unix_nano() — produces non-random sequences

decisions.decision: All three files share an identical input.generate + output.switch structure with only the mapping body varying
decisions.rationale: Uniformity lets the seed.yaml orchestrator run them identically; operators learn one config shape.

decisions.decision: Subject space is bounded by SEEDER_SUBJECT_COUNT rather than fully random UUIDs
decisions.rationale: Bounded subjects produce realistic per-subject meter aggregations in ClickHouse and let demo dashboards show meaningful per-customer breakdowns.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new seed stream for storage events
